prompt: As my lifts get stronger, my rest periods keep getting longer. What causes that, and what does the literature say about rest intervals for hypertrophy vs strength?
assertions: Explains that heavier loads increase total work and metabolic cost per set, requiring longer recovery
Notes that hypertrophy rest interval findings in research are mixed
Leans clearly toward longer rests for strength development, especially on compound lifts
Provides specific practical rest period ranges (e.g., ~90s-2min for upper/isolation, ~3min common, ~5min+ for heavy lower compounds)
Mentions supersets as a time-saving strategy without sacrificing key lift performance
